entries.name: FeeDisburser
entries.description: Contract used to disburse funds from system FeeVault contracts, shares revenue with Optimism and bridges the rest of funds to L1.

entries.name: L2CrossDomainMessenger
entries.description: The L2CrossDomainMessenger (L2xDM) contract sends messages from L2 to L1, and relays messages from L1 onto L2 with a system tx. In the event that a message sent from L2 to L1 is rejected for exceeding the L1 gas limit, it can be resubmitted via this contract’s replay function.

entries.name: L2StandardBridge
entries.description: The L2StandardBridge contract is the main entry point to deposit or withdraw ERC20 tokens from L2 to L1. This contract can store any token.

entries.name: SequencerFeeVault
entries.description: Collects the sequencer fees, which are withdrawable to the FeesCollector on L1.

entries.name: OptimismMintableERC20Factory
entries.description: Factory contract to create bridge compliant ERC20 IOU token representations of bridged L1 ERC20 tokens.

entries.name: L1BlockNumber
entries.description: Simple contract that returns the latest L1 block number.

entries.name: L2ERC721Bridge
entries.description: The L2ERC721Bridge contract is the main entry point to deposit or withdraw ERC721 tokens from L2 to L1. This contract can store any token.

entries.name: L1Block
entries.description: Simple contract that returns information about the latest L1 block, which is derived permissionlessly from the L1 chain.

entries.name: L2ToL1MessagePasser
entries.description: Contract used internally by the L2CrossDomainMessenger to send messages to L1, including withdrawals. It can also be used directly as a low-level interface.

entries.name: OptimismMintableERC721Factory
entries.description: Factory contract to create bridge compliant ERC721 IOU token representations of bridged L1 ERC721 tokens.

entries.name: BaseFeeVault
entries.description: Collects EIP-1559 base fees, which are withdrawable to the FeesCollector on L1.

entries.name: L1FeeVault
entries.description: Collects the L1 portion of the L2 transaction fees, which are withdrawable to the FeesCollector on L1.

entries.name: SchemaRegistry
entries.description: Contracts to register schemas for the Ethereum Attestation Service (EAS).

entries.name: EAS
entries.description: Contract containing the main logic for the Ethereum Attestation Service (EAS).

entries.name: HistoryStorage
entries.description: EIP-2935 system contract storing the last 8191 L1 blockhashes. Bytecode is canonical to the EIP, deployed at the same address across networks.

directlyReceivedPermissions.description: upgrading the bridge implementation can give access to all funds escrowed therein.

entries.name: OptimismMintableERC20Factory
entries.description: A helper contract that generates OptimismMintableERC20 contracts on the network it's deployed to. OptimismMintableERC20 is a standard extension of the base ERC20 token contract designed to allow the L1StandardBridge contracts to mint and burn tokens. This makes it possible to use an OptimismMintableERC20 as this chain's representation of a token on the host chain, or vice-versa.

entries.name: TimelockController
entries.description: A timelock with access control. The current minimum delay is 3d.

directlyReceivedPermissions.description: add/remove verifiers and the selectors they are mapped to.

receivedPermissions.description: it can update the preconfer address, the batch submitter (Sequencer) address and the gas configuration of the system.
can pause for up to 3mo 1d, but cannot unpause or extend pauses.

entries.name: TEEProverRegistry
entries.description: Registry of authorized TEE enclave signers and proposer addresses used by the TEEVerifier. Owner can add or remove allowlisted proposers via setProposer (onlyOwner) and set the AggregateVerifier game type lookup. Owner and Manager can register or deregister enclave signers via registerSigner / deregisterSigner. Registration requires a Risc0 ZK proof of a valid AWS Nitro attestation document verified by the NITRO_VERIFIER.

entries.name: AggregateVerifier
entries.description: Game type implementation that combines a TEE attestation arm and a ZK proof arm. A single un-nullified proof of either type can resolve a game (PROOF_THRESHOLD = 1). When both arms commit, the finalization window collapses from SLOW_FINALIZATION_DELAY (5d) to FAST_FINALIZATION_DELAY (1d).

entries.name: RiscZeroVerifierEmergencyStop
entries.description: A verifier wrapper for the eth:0xafB31f5b70623CDF4b20Ada3f7230916A5A79df9 that allows pausing (emergency stop) the verifier by its owner.

paused.description: verifier is stopped, cannot be used to verify new proofs or verify the integrity of receipts.

entries.name: PreimageOracle
entries.description: The PreimageOracle contract is used to load the required data from L1 for a dispute game.

entries.name: TEEVerifier
entries.description: Stateless verifier that validates AggregateVerifier TEE proofs by recovering an ECDSA signature over the journal and checking the recovered signer against TEEProverRegistry. Enforces PCR0 match by comparing the signer's registered image hash to the AggregateVerifier's TEE_IMAGE_HASH. Can be permanently nullified by a successful AggregateVerifier.nullify call.

entries.name: RiscZeroGroth16Verifier
entries.description: Verifier contract for RISC Zero Groth16 proofs (version 2.0.0-rc.3).

entries.name: RiscZeroGroth16Verifier
entries.description: Verifier contract for RISC Zero Groth16 proofs (version 3.0.0).

receivedPermissions.description: add/remove verifiers and the selectors they are mapped to.

entries.name: L1StandardBridge
entries.description: The main entry point to deposit ERC20 tokens from host chain to this chain.

entries.name: DisputeGameFactory
entries.description: The dispute game factory allows the creation of dispute games, used to propose state roots and eventually challenge them. This variant exposes per-type reads only; the legacy array views (gameImpls[], initBonds[]) were removed in the new implementation.

entries.name: RiscZeroVerifierEmergencyStop
entries.description: A verifier wrapper for the eth:0xf70aBAb028Eb6F4100A24B203E113D94E87DE93C that allows pausing (emergency stop) the verifier by its owner.

paused.description: verifier is stopped, cannot be used to verify new proofs or verify the integrity of receipts.

entries.name: OptimismPortal2
entries.description: The OptimismPortal contract is the main entry point to deposit funds from L1 to L2. It also allows to prove and finalize withdrawals. It specifies which game type can be used for withdrawals, which currently is the AggregateVerifier.

paused.description: Whether the contract is paused or not. Determined by the SuperchainConfig contract PAUSED_SLOT. Here it pauses withdrawals. If this is paused, also the L1CrossDomainMessenger and ERC-20, ERC-721 deposits are paused.

entries.name: RiscZeroSetVerifier
entries.description: Set verifier contract for RISC Zero proofs (version 0.9.0). It allows verifying a whole set of proofs identified with a Merkle root at once, afterwards each individual proof could be efficiently verified just by checking Merkle inclusion against the verified root.

receivedPermissions.description: Allowed to commit transactions from the current layer to the host chain.

entries.name: RiscZeroGroth16Verifier
entries.description: Verifier contract for RISC Zero Groth16 proofs (version 2.0.3).

entries.name: PermissionedDisputeGame
entries.description: Same as FaultDisputeGame, but only two permissioned addresses are designated as proposer and challenger.

entries.name: L1ERC721Bridge
entries.description: Used to bridge ERC-721 tokens from host chain to this chain.

entries.name: MIPS
entries.description: The MIPS contract is used to execute the final step of the dispute game which objectively determines the winner of the dispute.

entries.name: DelayedWETH
entries.description: Contract designed to hold the bonded ETH for each game. It is designed as a wrapper around WETH to allow an owner to function as a backstop if a game would incorrectly distribute funds.

entries.name: RiscZeroVerifierEmergencyStop
entries.description: A verifier wrapper for the eth:0x20ff7C2Cf391a5F096A2Cc181cb41916680f8E97 that allows pausing (emergency stop) the verifier by its owner.

paused.description: verifier is stopped, cannot be used to verify new proofs or verify the integrity of receipts.

entries.name: FaultDisputeGame
entries.description: Logic of the dispute game. When a state root is proposed, a dispute game contract is deployed. Challengers can use such contracts to challenge the proposed state root.

entries.name: SystemConfig
entries.description: Contains configuration parameters such as the Sequencer address, gas limit on this chain and the unsafe block signer address.

gasLimit.description: Gas limit for blocks on L2.

eip1559Denominator.description: volatility param: lower denominator -> quicker fee changes on L2

receivedPermissions.description: Allowed to pause withdrawals. In op stack systems with a proof system, the Guardian can also blacklist dispute games and set the respected game type (permissioned / permissionless).
affect the liveness and safety of the gateway - can transfer ownership, add and freeze verifier routes.
upgrading the bridge implementation can give access to all funds escrowed therein.

entries.name: Escrow
entries.description: Simple escrow that accepts tokens and allows to configure permissioned addresses that can access the tokens.

entries.name: NitroEnclaveVerifier
entries.description: ZK-based verifier of AWS Nitro Enclave attestation documents. Used by TEEProverRegistry to validate new enclave signer registrations against the AWS Nitro PKI.

zkConfigRiscZero.description: RiscZero (ZkCoProcessorType=1) config: single-attestation verifier program ID, batch aggregator program ID, and the default ZK verifier contract used when no per-selector route override applies.

zkConfigSuccinct.description: Succinct SP1 (ZkCoProcessorType=2) config: single-attestation verifier program ID, batch aggregator program ID, and the default ZK verifier contract used when no per-selector route override applies.

verifierProofIdRiscZero.description: verifier VK identifier matched against the batch journal during RiscZero (=1) aggregated verification.

verifierProofIdSuccinct.description: verifier VK identifier matched against the batch journal during Succinct SP1 (=2) aggregated verification.

zkVerifierRoutes.description: Per-(coprocessor, proof selector) verifier address overrides for the private _zkVerifierRoutes mapping, reconstructed from ZkRouteAdded logs with frozen routes (ZkRouteWasFrozen) removed. Empty means every coprocessor falls back to its default zkVerifier from zkConfig.

entries.name: RiscZeroVerifierEmergencyStop
entries.description: A verifier wrapper for the eth:0x5005aBa3DFf7C940fcc1e48DccCAD611a80eEB85 that allows pausing (emergency stop) the verifier by its owner.

paused.description: verifier is stopped, cannot be used to verify new proofs or verify the integrity of receipts.

entries.name: L1CrossDomainMessenger
entries.description: Sends messages from host chain to this chain, and relays messages back onto host chain. In the event that a message sent from host chain to this chain is rejected for exceeding this chain's epoch gas limit, it can be resubmitted via this contract's replay function.

entries.name: RiscZeroVerifierRouter
entries.description: A router proxy that routes to verifiers based on selectors. The mapping can be changed by a permissioned owner (eth:0x0b144E07A0826182B6b59788c34b32Bfa86Fb711).

entries.name: AddressManager
entries.description: Legacy contract used to manage a mapping of string names to addresses. Modern OP stack uses a different standard proxy system instead, but this contract is still necessary for backwards compatibility with several older contracts.

entries.name: AnchorStateRegistry
entries.description: Contains the latest confirmed state root that can be used as a starting point in a dispute game. This variant stores respectedGameType, retirementTimestamp, and disputeGameFinalityDelaySeconds locally and drops the legacy *FromGame fields, since the AggregateVerifier model does not expose vm()/weth()/absolutePrestate() on its game implementation.

entries.name: wstETHEscrow
entries.description: Escrow for custom external tokens that use the canonical bridge for messaging but are governed externally.

entries.name: RiscZeroVerifierEmergencyStop
entries.description: A verifier wrapper for the eth:0x2a098988600d87650Fb061FfAff08B97149Fa84D that allows pausing (emergency stop) the verifier by its owner.

paused.description: verifier is stopped, cannot be used to verify new proofs or verify the integrity of receipts.

entries.name: SkyLink Bridge
entries.description: Custom bridge for USDS and sUSDS managed by Sky governance.

entries.name: RiscZeroGroth16Verifier
entries.description: Verifier contract for RISC Zero Groth16 proofs (version 2.2.0).

entries.name: SuperchainConfig
entries.description: Base's own SuperchainConfig, used to manage pause states for the Base chain. The guardian and incident responder roles are immutable and set at construction time. The guardian can pause, unpause, and extend pauses, while the incident responder can only pause. Each pause automatically expires after 3 months.

entries.name: SP1Verifier
entries.description: Verifier contract for SP1 proofs (v6.1.0).

entries.name: ZkVerifier
entries.description: Thin router that forwards SP1 ZK proof verification from the AggregateVerifier game to the SP1 verifier gateway. Can be permanently nullified by a successful AggregateVerifier.nullify call.

entries.name: DelayedWETH
entries.description: Contract designed to hold the bonded ETH for each game. It is designed as a wrapper around WETH to allow an owner to function as a backstop if a game would incorrectly distribute funds.

entries.name: RiscZeroVerifierEmergencyStop
entries.description: A verifier wrapper for the eth:0x54aCE3ED46529B4d4F3770C8Bad5dDC48717B9bF that allows pausing (emergency stop) the verifier by its owner.

paused.description: verifier is stopped, cannot be used to verify new proofs or verify the integrity of receipts.

entries.name: SP1VerifierGateway
entries.description: This contract is the router for zk proof verification. It stores the mapping between identifiers and the address of onchain verifier contracts, routing each identifier to the corresponding verifier contract.

activeVerifiers.description: Verifiers that are routed to by their selector and not frozen.

allVerifiers.description: All verifiers that were ever routed to by this gateway.

entries.name: RiscZeroGroth16Verifier
entries.description: Verifier contract for RISC Zero Groth16 proofs. This older implementation exposes control-root and selector constants but does not expose a VERSION getter.
